steps.title: Compare the two views
steps.content: The question asks how the authors differ about the benefits of being named a City of Gastronomy. Text 1 says UNESCO helped Merida simply by selecting it, which frames the benefits as automatic. Text 2 says cities must create effective marketing strategies to benefit fully, so it frames the benefits as requiring intentional effort.

steps.title: Discard unsupported contrasts
steps.content:
• Choice A: Neither text compares how permanent the effects are.
  • Choice C: Text 2 does not question whether there has been any benefit; it says full benefit requires effort.
  • Choice D: Neither text says tour guides or tour operators are the main beneficiaries. Text 1 mentions local recipes, cooking practices, chefs, and tourism, while Text 2 focuses on marketing strategies for the city's food scene.
The answer is B.
